                                         Acknowledgement


    Five years have flown by in the blink of an eye. As I look back, these years can be roughly
summed up as three and a half years of relentless dedication, followed by a year and a half
of introspection and gradual liberation. As I prepare to move forward, I cannot help but feel
a sense of regret that my work, completed under time constraints, may lack the refinement it
deserves. I kindly ask for the understanding of my readers.

    Reflecting on my five-year journey of study and research, I would like to begin by express-
ing my deepest gratitude to my advisor, Prof. Qiang Li. Qiang cultivated a supportive, healthy,
and inspiring research environment for all his students, including myself. He showed tireless
dedication to our academic progress, research challenges, and even personal struggles, always
treating our ideas and opinions with respect and fairness. Under his guidance, I have grown
into an independent researcher with my own thoughts and aspirations. His visionary academic
insights, sharp thinking, and ability to nurture individual potential have always been a source
of admiration for me. Prof. Li shows the academic role model I aspire to follow throughout
my career.
    I am deeply grateful to all my colleagues in the CMS collaboration who have supported
me along the way. My heartfelt thanks go to my mentor, collaborator, and friend, Dr. Huilin
Qu, who guided me patiently as I started my journey into the CMS experiment and deep learn-
ing. You have shown me what it means to be a scientist driven by true ideals and passion. I
am profoundly thankful to my mentor during my research at CERN, Dr. Loukas Gouskos, for
his invaluable guidance on Higgs physics. My sincere thank goes to Dr. Santeri Laurila and
Dr. Michele Selvaggi for their invaluable advice and support in the VHcc research. I would also
like to thank Dr. Stephane Cooperstein, Dr. Raffaele Gerosa, Alexandre De Moor, and Sitian
Qian, for their guidance and insights during our early collaborations in deep learning topics.
My gratitude further extends to Prof. Javier Duarte, Dr. Raghav Kansal, and Dr. Cristina Man-
tilla Suarez for their collaborations, encouragement, and great support across various research
subjets. To my peers and colleagues in the Peking group—Antonis Agapitos, Ying An, Cheng
Chen, Sen Deng, Zhe Guan, Qilong Guo, Andrew Levin, Yangu Li, Meng Lu, Xudong Lyu,
Jing Peng, Sitian Qian, Yunxuan Song, Jie Xiao, Tianyi Yang, Yuzhe Zhao, and many others—
thank you for your academic support and encouragement during countless discussions. I would
also like to express my gratitude to Prof. Yajun Mao, Prof. Xiaohu Sun, and Prof. Chen Zhou
for inspiring me through thought-provoking conversations.
    My gratitude extends to all the CMS collaborators who taught me how to contribute to
large-scale experiments, collaborate effectively, and integrate within a team. I want to give
                                             277
                                 Peking University Doctoral Dissertation


special thanks to Alexander Grohsjean, Meng Lu, and my colleagues from the CMS BTV
team—Sen Deng, Svenja Diekmann, Ming-Yan Lee, Jan van der Linden, Spandan Mondal,
Alexandre De Moor, and Sebastian Wuchterl—for their generous support and patience. It has
been an honour to work alongside all of you and to grow together as a team.
    I would also like to thank the colleagues and classmates with whom I collaborated on
various projects during my Ph.D. To Fanqiang Meng—our early collaborations and passionate
discussions remain a treasured memory. To Sen Deng and Dawei Fu—working with you taught
me the value of diligence and precision. To Jingshu Li—thank you for your understanding,
patience, and support during our teamwork. I am also grateful to my younger colleagues—
Leyun Gao, Zixun Kou, Haonan Lu, Zhenxuan Zhang, and Yuzhe Zhao—for the enriching
idea exchanges that pushed me to grow further.
    I owe my deepest gratitude to Peking University. Over the past nine years, its open, in-
clusive, and vibrant academic atmosphere has significantly shaped my character and nurtured
my passion for knowledge—I am thankful to all the professors, administrative staff, mentors,
and classmates I have had the privilege to meet here. I would also like to express my thanks
to the China Scholarship Council for their financial support, which enabled me to conduct my
research at CERN and engage closely with international collaborators.
    I am immensely grateful to my friends and peers who supported me throughout these
years. To Haipeng Lin, Yue Liu, Tong Ying, and Linfeng Zhang—your encouragement, es-
pecially during the pandemic, meant so much to me. To Ziyang Zhang—thank you for your
unwavering support and care from afar. I also deeply treasure the life spent with my former
roommate, Yunxuan Song, and the warmth and companionship of my roommates at CERN—
Jingyi Han, Jinfeng Liu, Xuelong Qin, Mingtao Zhang, and Yongke Zhao—who made life
easier and brighter with their kindness.
    Finally, my heartfelt thanks go to Yannan Pan. Your warmth, encouragement, and unwa-
vering support have been my greatest source of strength over the past ten years, giving me the
courage and determination to face every challenge. To my parents, whose unconditional love
and nurturing have shaped who I am today, I owe everything—without you, I could never have
reached this point in my life.

    I would also like to take this opportunity to express my deep gratitude for the longstanding
dedication to my ideals. This persistence may have taken root in my youth through a love of
physics, emerged on that very day after the Gaokao when I encountered The Last Question
and felt an overwhelming sense of purpose, or arisen from a profound resonance with Death
                                                  278
                                            Acknowledgement


for Truth (Zhao Wen Dao). I am indebted to my childhood friends, mentors, and strangers I
have never met, whose presence has quietly shaped my life. Indeed, the journey of scientific
research and collaboration is often fraught with challenges and hidden obstacles. Yet, this very
persistence has quietly carried me through adversity. For this, I extend my heartfelt gratitude
to the world, which continues to offer its silent support as I press forward.
    Forge ahead. May you look back fifty years from now and still declare with the golden
conviction.
        The vast unobstructed firmament
        Is lit with glimmering stars;
        They are a new turning:
        These hieroglyphs of the ages,
        These staring eyes of the future.
        (Excerpt from Bei Dao’s poem The Answer)
                                                                         Congqiao Li
                                                                   June 2024, at Yanyuan
